dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 658 : To amend title 38, United States Code, to establish qualifications for the appointment of a person as a marriage and family therapist, qualified to provide clinical supervision, in the Veterans Health Administration.
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Received; read twice and referred to the Committee on Veterans' Affairs

form.official-title: To amend title 38, United States Code, to establish qualifications for the appointment of a person as a marriage and family therapist, qualified to provide clinical supervision, in the Veterans Health Administration.

section.header: Establishment of qualifications for the appointment of a person as a marriage and family therapist, qualified to provide clinical supervision, in the Veterans Health Administration
section.text: Section 7402(b)(10) of title 38, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: by redesignating subparagraphs (A) and (B) as clauses (i) and (ii), respectively;
by inserting (A) before To be eligible; and
by adding at the end the following new subparagraph (B):

subparagraph.text: To be eligible to be appointed to a marriage and family therapist position and qualified to provide clinical supervision, a person must—

clause.text: have the qualifications set forth in subparagraph (A); and
be recognized by the State described in clause (ii) of such subparagraph as a provider of, or otherwise allowed by such State to provide, clinical supervision.

attestation-group.attestation-date: Passed the House of Representatives February 2, 2026.
